notes: Patterns ported from the original Cashiro MPESAParser.kt (parser-core/src/main/kotlin/com/ritesh/parser/core/bank/MPESAParser.kt). M-PESA (Safaricom Kenya) mobile-money wallet: every transaction message starts with a 10-char alphanumeric transaction ID followed by "Confirmed" (or "Congratulations! <ID> confirmed" for B2C payouts) and carries a "New M-PESA balance is Ksh..." trailer. Notes vs the Kotlin original: - Kotlin requires "confirmed" AND one of paid to/sent to/received/new m-pesa balance; the manifest filter only supports a single OR-list, so we require "confirmed" (the strict gate against promos) — messages with "confirmed" but no transaction keywords still fail amount/type extraction. - Kotlin checks income keywords before expense; the engine's typeRules priority is expense-before-income, so income is set via a pipeline setFieldWhen step to preserve the original order. - Paybill messages ("...for account 123123") inherit the Kotlin base-class account extraction (last 4 of the paybill account number); person-to-person and received messages have no account, so they fall back to "0000" like other wallet providers (see jiopay.ts rationale). - Kotlin's MPESAParser.cleanMerchantName deliberately keeps "LIMITED"/"LTD" in company names, so the cleaning rules here do NOT strip them.

fixtures.body: TJK6H7T3GA Confirmed. Ksh70.00 paid to person 1. on 20/10/24 at 4:21 PM.New M-PESA balance is Ksh123.12. Transaction cost, Ksh0.00. Amount you can transact within the day is 499,895.00. Save frequent Tills for quick payment on M-PESA app https://bit.ly/mpesalnk
